task_completion.description: Reduce the solar absorptance of 'EWall Construction' to 0.45
task_completion.visual_evidence: eQUEST Construction properties window or spreadsheet view for 'EWall Construction' showing the Absorptance value changed to 0.45.

task_completion.description: Reduce the solar absorptance of 'Roof Construction' to 0.45
task_completion.visual_evidence: eQUEST Construction properties window or spreadsheet view for 'Roof Construction' showing the Absorptance value changed to 0.45.

task_completion.description: Set the dry-bulb economizer temperature limit to 65°F for all five Ground Floor HVAC systems
task_completion.visual_evidence: eQUEST Air-Side HVAC System properties window or spreadsheet view showing the Economizer Drybulb Limit (or High Limit) set to 65 for the Ground Floor systems (e.g., 'Sys1 (PSZ) (G.S11)', 'G.E12', 'G.N13', 'G.W14', 'G.C15').

task_completion.description: Run a full annual DOE-2.2 simulation
task_completion.visual_evidence: eQUEST 'Simulation in Progress' window, a 'Simulation Complete' dialog, or the agent clicking the 'Perform Simulation' button.
Agent clicking 'File -> Save', clicking the save (floppy disk) icon on the toolbar, or a save confirmation dialog appearing.

integrity.description: Agent used the eQUEST GUI to modify the building model
integrity.visual_evidence: Changes should be made through eQUEST's interface (dialog boxes, spreadsheets), not by opening and editing the .inp file in a text editor like Notepad.

integrity.description: Simulation was executed within the eQUEST application
integrity.visual_evidence: The DOE-2.2 simulation progress window should be visible in eQUEST, rather than terminal commands or mocked outputs.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target building is '4StoreyBuilding'. The baseline solar absorptance is 0.6 for both 'EWall Construction' and 'Roof Construction', which must be changed to 0.45. There are exactly five ground-floor packaged single-zone HVAC systems (G.S11, G.E12, G.N13, G.W14, G.C15). Their baseline dry-bulb economizer limit (DRYBULB-LIMIT) is 70°F, which must be changed to 65°F. A DOE-2.2 simulation must be run and the project saved.
